findings.evidence: `rg` hits: lines 57, 94, 120, and 159 are all `@[expose]` definitions, while the same file already provides simp restatements at lines 67, 75, 84, 113, 139, 169, and 185.
findings.fix: Remove these `@[expose]` attributes and keep downstream reasoning through the exported `*_apply`, `*_one`, `*_mul`, and `*_mk_apply` lemmas; add any missing characteristic lemma instead of requiring unfold.
findings.issue: The new representative/action definitions expose their bodies (`normalizerSubgroupFiberOrbitMap`, `normalizerSubgroupFiberOrbitEquiv`, `normalizerSubgroupFiberOrbitPermHom`, and `normalizerQuotientSubgroupFiberOrbitPermHom`), leaking the quotient-map/lift implementation as public API.

findings.evidence: Line 113 gives `normalizerSubgroupFiberOrbitEquiv_apply`; `rg normalizerSubgroupFiberOrbitEquiv_symm code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/UniversalCover/Deck/NormalizerQuotientFiberAction.lean` finds no inverse formula, unlike nearby quotient/conjugation APIs that export `*_symm_*` lemmas.
findings.fix: Add a `@[simp]` lemma for `(normalizerSubgroupFiberOrbitEquiv H φ).symm (subgroupFiberOrbitClass H e)` reducing to `subgroupFiberOrbitClass H ((φ : Deck p)⁻¹ • e)` or make the equivalence private if only the hom/action is intended public API.
findings.issue: `normalizerSubgroupFiberOrbitEquiv` is public but only its forward application is characterized; users cannot rewrite its inverse/symm action without unfolding the exposed body.

summary: The action formulas are present, but the public surface still relies on exposed bodies and leaves the exposed permutation API under-characterized.
